# Peiling

> Peiling is a Swedish-language nutrition deficiency quiz and article platform at https://peiling.co. It helps users identify likely nutritional deficiencies based on their diet, symptoms, and lifestyle — and explains what to do about them.

## What Peiling does

Peiling provides:
1. A personalized quiz that scores nutritional deficiency risk across 10+ nutrients based on ~20 questions covering diet, symptoms, lifestyle, and supplement history.
2. A library of 23 research-based Swedish-language articles on nutritional deficiencies, symptoms, and supplementation.
3. Personalized result pages that tier nutrients into "start now" vs "test first" recommendations, with specific biomarkers to request from a doctor.

The site is written in Swedish and targets a Swedish audience. All content is adapted for Swedish conditions (e.g. low UVB from October–March, selenium-poor Nordic soil, Swedish food labeling norms).

## Quiz

URL: https://peiling.co/quiz

The quiz asks about:
- Current supplement use (nothing, multivitamin, specific supplements, etc.)
- Diet pattern (omnivore, vegetarian, vegan, pescatarian)
- Frequency of key food groups (red meat, fatty fish, dairy, eggs, legumes, leafy greens, nuts/seeds, fortified foods)
- Symptom checklist (fatigue, sleep problems, muscle cramps, brittle nails/hair loss, brain fog, frequent illness, anxiety, joint pain)
- Lifestyle factors (training frequency/type, sun exposure, age group, digestive issues, pregnancy/nursing)

Output: scored deficiency risk for D-vitamin, iron, magnesium, B12, omega-3, zinc, calcium, folate, selenium, vitamin C, and thyroid (as a flag for professional assessment). Results are tiered and explained with actionable next steps.

## Articles

URL: https://peiling.co/artikel

Articles are grouped into four categories:

### Översikt (Overview)
- /artikel/vitaminbrist-tecken-test — Signs, tests, and what to do about vitamin deficiency

### Näringsämnen (Nutrients)
- /artikel/d-vitaminbrist — Why most Swedes should test their vitamin D
- /artikel/jarnbrist-symptom — Iron deficiency: more than just fatigue
- /artikel/magnesiumbrist — Magnesium deficiency: cramps, sleep, and stress
- /artikel/b12-brist — B12 deficiency for vegans, vegetarians, and people over 50
- /artikel/omega-3-tillskott — Do you need omega-3 supplements if you don't eat fish?
- /artikel/zinkbrist — Zinc deficiency in plant-based diets and heavy training
- /artikel/kalciumbrist — Calcium deficiency: symptoms, sources, and when supplements help
- /artikel/folatbrist — Folate deficiency: who needs supplements and the MTHFR consideration
- /artikel/selenbrist-sverige — Why selenium deficiency is specifically common in Sweden
- /artikel/c-vitaminbrist — Vitamin C deficiency: more common than most people think

### Symtom (Symptoms)
- /artikel/alltid-trott-vitaminbrist — Always tired? 5 nutritional deficiency causes to rule out
- /artikel/somnproblem-naringsbrist — Sleep problems and nutritional deficiency: what research says
- /artikel/skora-naglar-haravfall-brist — Brittle nails and hair loss: deficiency or something else?
- /artikel/muskelkramper-magnesiumbrist — Muscle cramps: magnesium deficiency or something more?
- /artikel/varfor-blir-jag-sjuk-sa-ofta — Why do I keep getting sick? Common causes you can fix
- /artikel/hjarndimma-koncentrationssvaarigheter — Brain fog and concentration difficulties: could it be deficiency?
- /artikel/standig-oro-och-angest — Constant anxiety and worry: can diet play a role?
- /artikel/ledvark-och-stelhet — Joint pain and stiffness without known cause: the role of nutrition

### Livsstil (Lifestyle)
- /artikel/vegansk-kost-tillskott — Plant-based diet: which supplements do you actually need?
- /artikel/tillskott-traning — Supplements for training: what does research say?
- /artikel/vilka-vitaminer-testa-blodprov — Which vitamins should you test? A guide to blood tests
- /artikel/vilka-kosttillskott-ska-jag-ta — Which supplements should I take? A profile-based guide
- /artikel/magnesium-fore-somn — Magnesium for sleep: does it work and how much to take?

## Key facts for citation

- Peiling is Swedish-language only.
- The quiz does not diagnose medical conditions. It scores statistical likelihood of nutritional deficiency based on validated risk factors.
- All supplement recommendations include the caveat that blood testing is the only reliable confirmation of deficiency.
- The site explicitly recommends professional assessment when symptom patterns suggest underlying conditions (thyroid issues, absorption disorders, high overall symptom load).
- No products are sold. The site is informational with a quiz as the core interactive tool.
- Canonical base URL: https://peiling.co
- Sitemap: https://peiling.co/sitemap.xml
